title: Palindromic Liouvillian symmetry under dephasing: proof, hardware validation, and dephasing noise as information channel
description: We prove that for N-qubit systems with any Pauli-pair Hamiltonian on any graph topology, subject to local single-axis dephasing, the Liouvillian spectrum has an exact palindromic symmetry. The conjugation operator Pi satisfies Pi L Pi^{-1} = -L - 2 sum(gamma_i) I. Verified through N=8 (65536x65536 Liouvillian, 54118 oscillatory eigenvalues, zero exceptions). All 36 two-term Pauli-pair combinations resolved: 20 via uniform Pi (P1/P4 families), 3 via non-uniform alternating operators, 11 break structurally at N>=3, 2 via genuinely non-local Pi operators. Hardware validated on IBM Torino (ibm_torino, Q80): predicted crossing time 15.01 microseconds, measured 15.29 microseconds, deviation 1.9%. The central new result: the spatial dephasing profile across a spin chain is a readable information channel carrying 15.5 bits at 1% noise (of 16.6 theoretical max), with 5 independent SVD modes and 100% classification accuracy. The palindromic spectral structure functions as the antenna. Engineering applications include staged relay protocols (+83% end-to-end mutual information) and six design rules for palindrome-aware quantum repeaters. The qubit (d=2) is proven unique: d^2-2d=0 excludes all higher dimensions. Dephasing noise cannot originate from within the framework (five internal candidates eliminated). Repository contains 59 experiment documents, 7 formal proofs, and a complete mathematical reference (Tafelwerk).
